title: Making A Large Island
hints: Consider the island sizes if no flip occurred. How can you efficiently identify which cell belongs to which island?
Label each existing island with a unique ID and store its size. Then, for every 0, check its neighbors' IDs to calculate the potential new island size.

Two traps decide correctness: a single 0 may border the same island on more than one side, so each distinct neighboring island should be counted only once; and if the grid contains no 0 at all, the answer is just the largest island as it stands. Joining a flipped cell to its distinct neighbors is where the size grows.
